note: The two trees framing the Serenity Garden turn vermillion in early summer -- visible from across the park.
quick_hits: When a Royal Poinciana blooms, it earns its other name — Flamboyant Tree. The canopy can be entirely covered in vermillion flowers, visible from blocks away.

note: Covered in orange-red tubes by the office -- watch for hummingbirds and zebra longwings.
quick_hits: Firebush is one of the most important native plants in South Florida for wildlife — hummingbirds, butterflies, and more than two dozen bird species use it for nectar, fruit, or cover through the entire year.

note: Roosting in a fruit tree near the big pond in the Rare Fruit Trail for a week or two. They hide well and blend into the branches as a natural defense.
quick_hits: Despite the name, the screech-owl does not screech. Its usual call is a soft, descending whinny or an even, trilling purr — a gentle sound from such a capable little hunter.

note: Very cool red stars topped with a blueberry. Gotta check that out
quick_hits: The real attraction is not the flower but what follows it — a large, waxy, inflated calyx in deep red-purple that persists and swells as the fruit develops inside. The calyx is the show.
